mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 5-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 4.0%, labor market slack signals weakening consumer demand and higher recession probability, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business investment strength, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC above 0.04 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side contraction and margin pressure, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods yield positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the signal predictive power vanishes when FEDFUNDS is rising.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5%, restrictive monetary policy compresses equity valuations and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 70% of observations where FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.5% over a 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS increase and UNRATE decrease fails to produce positive 60-day SPX returns with a t-statistic above 1.96 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real GDP directly expands corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show a negative or zero correlation with 20-day SPX forward returns across all tested rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls below the 100 baseline index, manufacturing contraction signals deteriorating economic momentum, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where INDPRO drops below 100 over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
